text.title: Is This The Most Beautiful Country In The World? Ultimate 10 Day Norway Travel Guide
text.u: Norway is truly one of the world's most
underrated travel destinations
despite having periods of 24-hour
Darkness Norwegians are consistently
ranked as some of the happiest people on
earth
with breathtaking fjords jaw-dropping
Cliffs and an abundance of natural
waterfalls and lakes Norway is truly one
of Nature's masterpieces
[Music]
foreign
[Music]
come with us to find out why Norwegians
are some of the happiest people on Earth
and why you need to visit the
spectacular country
we started our trip to the breathtaking
country of Norway in the small town of
Buddha located just above the Arctic
Circle we spent the morning exploring
and walking around the town before
beginning our tour to the world's
largest and most powerful whirlpool
salstromen
this dangerous natural phenomenon is
what many fishermen thought was a
Viking's own sea monster
[Music]
tour began by checking out the local
Eagle families which was honestly
incredible
[Music]
after witnessing The Majestic and
Powerful Eagles we made our way to
Saul's throngen
[Music]
[Music]
as we rode through the water it was
incredible and a bit Eerie to feel the
power of the current at times it didn't
feel like our small boat would have the
power to escape the grasps of this
powerful maelstrom
visiting saus throughman was an
awe-inspiring and humbly experience to
feel the sheer power of Mother Nature
and it was a fantastic way to start our
trip
[Music]
after our tortoises through men we
walked across town to take a three-hour
ferry ride to lofit
upon arrival we got our rental car and
drove just 10 minutes to the town of O
where we stayed for the night
for our second full Day in Norway we set
out to explore the picturesque Lofoten
Islands but many people think of Norway
they think of Lofoten known for stunning
Landscapes and the 24-hour sunlight in
the summer Lofoten truly is an iconic
travel destination
we started our morning exploring the
fishing town of oh and learning about
the stock fish industry which played a
major role in the history of Lofoten
[Music]
we then headed just 20 minutes down the
road to the city of Reina for the iconic
reinabregan hike while this hike was
challenging it was certainly worth it
and provided what Jason and I both
agreed was the most beautiful view from
a hike that we had ever seen
foreign
[Music]
we had a delicious lunch at a cafe in
town and headed an hour and a half north
across the beautiful Norwegian landscape
our first stop was skogsonden for a
second while we were sitting in the car
and looking out we couldn't tell if we
were in Hawaii or in Norway
the beautiful white sand beach was a
complete contrast from the mountainous
terrain we had just experienced which
made it all the more beautiful
[Music]
after skogson then we drove 30 minutes
more to the picturesque town of newsford
[Music]
news Fjord is a quaint town along the
inlet of the Fjord famously known for
its yellow fishing huts
foreign
[Music]
we headed to our hotel both exhausted
and energized from our full day of
Adventure on lofitin
[Music]
our first activity the next day was to
hike floya in spoolver IA was probably
our most challenging hike in Lofoten but
it was definitely worth it for the
breathtaking 360 degree views of
Northern Lofoten
[Music]
after our hike we drove 30 minutes to
the city of henningsfair for a look at
what we would consider to be the most
beautiful football pitch in the entire
world unfortunately the stunning city
was our last stop in Lofoten
Jason and I absolutely loved lopotin
everywhere we went our Jaws dropped in
awe of Mother Nature and the incredible
natural beauty that surrounded us we
weren't sure if it would live up to all
the hype of the pictures but now we can
say for sure that it looks even better
in person of all of our trip we wish we
could have spent more time exploring
Lofoten
we began our day in also with a
delicious Norwegian breakfast
here in Norway and it's amazing got some
bread with jam and brown cheese
fantastic some moosely and then some
smoked salmon with cheeses it's
[Music]
after a breakfast for the Champions we
headed to a much needed Norwegian sauna
after two hard days of hiking and
exploring
[Music]
okay so we just did our first round in
the sauna I've done saunas in America
but they're like not as hot as stuff
it's so hot like two minutes and I'm
dying and I'm about to jump in the water
here we go
[Music]
foreign
[Music]
this was an absolute blast and
Incredibly relaxing in our books this is
a must-do activity when visiting Norway
[Music]
okay so update after the sauna we feel
amazing I feel so relaxed feel really
good seriously I need to bring one of
these back to the States because that
was awesome after the sauna we had some
lunch and set out to explore the modern
yet relaxed city of Oslo
[Music]
foreign
[Music]
foreign
after a fun-filled day shopping relaxing
and exploring we headed to Oslo Central
Station to take the overnight train to
Stavanger
neither Jason or I had ever done a
sleeper train before and so we were sort
of nervous but also excited for what
felt like a childhood dream of a
sleepover on a train
[Music]
okay so we got ready for bed and had the
flights off and everything we're just
gonna try and go to sleep it's kind of
nice because the train ride is eight
hours and 50 minutes hopefully should be
able to get some solid eight hours of
shut eye and wake up refresh for our day
tomorrow instabonger
after arriving in Stavanger we spent the
morning exploring the beautiful city
center before grabbing our rental car
and heading to the infamous Pulpit Rock
hike
this hike is where Mission Impossible
was filmed and is one of Norway's most
popular hikes
[Music]
after around two and a half hours of
moderate hiking we made it to the top
the views from the top were Sublime but
a little scary at the same time
walking along the cliff to the final
Viewpoint we were within feet of what
looked like a thousand foot drop
okay so we made it up to the top of
Pulpit Rock and it's absolutely gorgeous
but very busy as you can see the line
behind us of people yeah when they
thought it was crowded like that's no
joke like you can see the line to get
your picture there's people all over we
were passing people on the trail so uh
just know it's pretty busy
foreign
[Music]
after our hike we headed down for some
delicious K Bob okay so very few things
taste better than a kebab after a long
hike in Norway
okay
after lunch we drove four hours through
the beautiful Norwegian Countryside to
the city of Oda
along the way we made several stops just
to take in the stunning nature all
around us
while neither Rachel or I were really
looking forward to the drive we
absolutely love seeing the countryside
and both agreed that it was one of the
most beautiful road trips that we have
ever taken
[Music]
foreign
[Music]
we had dinner and hit the hay early
nervous for what would be the longest
hike of Our Lives the next morning
[Music]
okay so today is our day that we are
going to hike trolltunga and of course
as we wake up it's just like pouring
rain
and supposed to be raining all day today
so I'll be honest I'm not
super excited to do it right now but
hopefully the views and everything will
be worth it we'll be able to stay
um some will dry with the gear that we
got
after yet another delicious Norwegian
breakfast we made our way in the rain to
the base of trolltunga
while the hike was tough and the rain
didn't help it wasn't actually as bad as
we thought it would be
after just over four hours we made it to
the impressive trolls tongue outcropping
just like Pulpit Rock the sheer
drop-offs were a little bit terrifying
[Music]
thankfully the clouds cleared up just in
time for us to get our photos
[Music]
despite our fears our day at trotunga
was awesome it was a reminder to us of
what a privilege it is to have good
health and the rush of conquering our
fears and stepping outside of our
comfort zone
looking back we are so glad we showed up
that morning even when we didn't feel
like it
after trolltunga we drove an hour and a
half down the road to Vols where we
stayed for the night
the next morning
Lord the city of both
[Music]
after a relaxing morning in Vost we took
an hour-long bus ride to the beautiful
countryside city of Flom
[Music]
is home to the famous phloem Railway
which is said to be one of the most
beautiful railroads in the world
the Charming shops have flown made us
feel like we were walking in a fairy
tale after doing some shopping it was
our turn to board the train okay so here
we are on the actual train Pro tip if
you come is sit on the right side we've
been told the right side has better
views they're both incredible but
they're right it would be slightly
better so that's going from Plum to
Myrtle you'll want to be on the right
side
[Music]
thank you
foreign
[Music]
[Music]
Railway were certainly beautiful we
didn't feel like it was anything special
in comparison to the rest of our travel
by car or bus
regardless it was a beautiful and
relaxing way to travel to our next and
Final Destination of Bergen
we start our day in Bergen with
breakfast at our hotel which was yet
again absolutely delicious one of the
things we found out during our trip was
that hotel chains have a competition
each year to see who can have the best
breakfast for that reason the breakfast
at hotels throughout Norway is some of
the best in the world
one thing you absolutely need to try
when visiting is Norwegian waffles with
jam and brown cheese while it sounds
like an odd combo it is absolutely
delicious and honestly probably the
highlight of our food experience in
Norway
[Music]
after breakfast we set out to explore
the picturesque town of Berrigan and to
do some shopping for Norwegian souvenirs
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
foreign
[Music]
cable cars were closed for maintenance
so we made our way to the Foy bonnen for
a beautiful sunset and panoramic view of
Bergen
thank you
[Music]
thank you
as we watch the sunset we reflected on
the incredible vacation we had Norway
was absolutely stunning
the breathtaking fjords
bass mountain ranges jaw-dropping Cliffs
and hikes and resilient Norwegian people
make Norway a place that we truly love
travel truly is one of life's most
fulfilling experiences
as always we are so grateful for the
privilege that we had to visit such an
amazing country and for the little
things that we took and incorporated
into our lives there is a sense of
balance throughout the entire country
and in the people which just feels right
[Music]
throughout our trip we truly began to
see why Norwegians are some of the
happiest people on Earth if you have the
opportunity we would definitely
recommend taking a trip to Norway to
experience all that has to offer for
yourself
[Music]
hey everyone thanks so much for watching
we really hope you enjoyed this video if
you did it would really help us out if
you gave us a like and subscribed we
have tons more travel content we are
excited to show you as always we hope
you have an amazing day
[Music]
